text.title: Null Safety Patterns
text.summary: Patterns for handling null, undefined, and array out-of-bounds under strictNullChecks, noUncheckedIndexedAccess, and exactOptionalPropertyTypes.

rule.statement: Use unknown instead of any for values of unverified external origin; narrow before using.
rule.rationale: unknown forces explicit type checking before access; any silently permits all operations including property access on undefined.

rule.statement: Return T | undefined from functions that can produce no result; throw with a message when the caller guarantees the value exists.
rule.rationale: Returning T without null handling silently crashes at runtime; T | undefined forces the caller to handle the absent case.

rule.statement: When accessing array elements by index, handle the T | undefined case before using the value.
rule.rationale: Without this flag, arr[5] on a string[] has type string even though it may be undefined; the flag adds | undefined to make the risk explicit.

rule.statement: Omit optional properties rather than setting them to undefined; add | undefined to the type annotation when explicit undefined must be assignable.
rule.rationale: exactOptionalPropertyTypes distinguishes "property absent" from "property present with value undefined" — a meaningful semantic difference for serialization and Object.keys enumeration.
